record.field: Chatwoot is an open-source, omnichannel customer engagement platform that unifies every support conversation in a single shared inbox.

Chatwoot is an open-source, omnichannel customer engagement platform that unifies every support conversation in a single shared inbox.

It consolidates live chat, email, WhatsApp, Instagram, Facebook Messenger, Telegram, SMS and a custom API channel into one collaborative workspace, positioning itself as a self-hostable alternative to Intercom and Zendesk. Built on Ruby on Rails and Vue.js, it follows an open-core model: the MIT-licensed Community Edition is free to self-host, while enterprise features are offered separately. It targets support, sales and success teams that want to own their customer data on their own infrastructure.

Key features
  • Shared team inbox aggregating live chat, email and social/messaging channels
  • Embeddable website live-chat widget with an SDK and a self-service help center
  • Captain AI agent for automated replies, plus canned responses, labels and macros
  • Auto-assignment, agent capacity rules, teams, private notes and @mentions
  • Contact management, segmentation, CSAT surveys and reporting/analytics
  • Webhooks, REST API and integrations (Slack, Shopify, Dialogflow, Linear)
Best for

Support, sales and customer-success teams that need an omnichannel help desk and live chat while keeping conversations and customer data on self-hosted infrastructure.

field.name: tag_ids
field.eval: [Command.set([ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_self_hosted'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_communication'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_chat'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_crm'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_collaboration'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_ai'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_email'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_helpdesk')])]

record.field: services: chatwoot-rails: image: chatwoot/chatwoot:v4.0.3 volumes: - ./chatwoot_storage:/app/storage environment: &id001 - FRONTEND_URL=${FRONTEND_URL} - SECRET_KEY_BASE=${SECRET_KEY_BASE} - RAILS_ENV=${RAILS_ENV} - NODE_ENV=${NODE_ENV} - INSTALLATION_ENV=${INSTALLATION_ENV} - RAILS_LOG_TO_STDOUT=${RAILS_LOG_TO_STDOUT} - LOG_LEVEL=${LOG_LEVEL} - DEFAULT_LOCALE=${DEFAULT_LOCALE} - POSTGRES_HOST=${POSTGRES_HOST} - POSTGRES_PORT=${POSTGRES_PORT} - POSTGRES_DATABASE=${POSTGRES_DATABASE} - POSTGRES_USERNAME=${POSTGRES_USERNAME} - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=${POSTGRES_PASSWORD} - REDIS_URL=${REDIS_URL} - ENABLE_ACCOUNT_SIGNUP=${ENABLE_ACCOUNT_SIGNUP} - ACTIVE_STORAGE_SERVICE=${ACTIVE_STORAGE_SERVICE} depends_on: chatwoot-postgres: condition: service_started chatwoot-redis: condition: service_started entrypoint: docker/entrypoints/rails.sh command: - bundle - exec - sh - -c - rails db:chatwoot_prepare && rails s -p 3000 -b 0.0.0.0 restart: always container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:3000' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network chatwoot-sidekiq: image: chatwoot/chatwoot:v4.0.3 volumes: - ./chatwoot_storage:/app/storage environment: *id001 depends_on: chatwoot-postgres: condition: service_started chatwoot-redis: condition: service_started command: - bundle - exec - sidekiq - -C - config/sidekiq.yml restart: always container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_chatwoot_sidekiq' networks: - internal chatwoot-postgres: image: pgvector/pgvector:pg14 restart: always volumes: - ./chatwoot_postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data - ./init-vector.sql:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/init-vector.sql environment: - POSTGRES_DB=${POSTGRES_DATABASE} - POSTGRES_USER=${POSTGRES_USERNAME} - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=${POSTGRES_PASSWORD} container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_chatwoot_postgres' networks: - internal chatwoot-redis: image: redis:alpine restart: always volumes: - ./chatwoot_redis_data:/data container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_chatwoot_redis' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true
